title: Shortest Path in a Binary Matrix (8 Directions)
description:
Given an n×n binary matrix grid where 0 means empty and 1 means blocked, return the length of the shortest clear path from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1) moving in 8 directions. Return -1 if no such path exists.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 200
explanation:
Intuition
Unweighted shortest path on a grid is solved with BFS. Each step expands neighbors in all 8 directions.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Check start and end are open
2. Main Algorithm: BFS from (0,0) marking distance
3. Return Result: Return distance at first arrival to target else -1

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2)
- Space Complexity: O(n^2)

Key Insights
- Push neighbors only if inside bounds and zero
- Mark visited when enqueuing to avoid repeats

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting diagonals leads to wrong path length
- Not handling blocked start or end
